source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
title: Ensure IAM policies that allow full "*:*" administrative privileges are not attached

input_row.source: CIS AMAZON WEB SERVICES FOUNDATIONS BENCHMARK V5.0.0
input_row.title: Ensure IAM policies that allow full "*:*" administrative privileges are not attached
input_row.description: IAM policies are the means by which privileges are granted to users, groups, or roles. It is recommended and considered standard security advice to grant least privilege—that is, granting only the permissions required to perform a task. Determine what users need to do, and then craft policies for them that allow the users to perform only those tasks, instead of granting full administrative privileges.
input_row.rationale: It's more secure to start with a minimum set of permissions and grant additional permissions as necessary, rather than starting with permissions that are too lenient and then attempting to tighten them later. Providing full administrative privileges instead of restricting access to the minimum set of permissions required for the user exposes resources to potentially unwanted actions. IAM policies that contain a statement with "Effect": "Allow" and "Action": "*" over "Resource": "*" should be removed.
input_row.audit: Perform the following to determine existing policies: From Command Line: 1. Run the following to get a list of IAM policies: aws iam list-policies --only-attached --output text 2. For each policy returned, run the following command to determine if any policy is allowing full administrative privileges on the account: aws iam get-policy-version  --policy-arn <policy_arn> --version-id <version> 3. In the output, the policy should not contain any Statement block with "Effect": "Allow" and Action set to "*" and Resource set to "*".
input_row.remediation: From Console: Perform the following to detach the policy that has full administrative privileges: 1. Sign in to the AWS Management Console and open the IAM console at https://console.aws.amazon.com/iam/. 2. In the navigation pane, click Policies and then search for the policy name found in the audit step. 3. Select the policy that needs to be deleted. 4. In the policy action menu, select Detach. 5. Select all Users, Groups, Roles that have this policy attached. 6. Click Detach Policy. 7. Select the newly detached policy and select Delete. From Command Line: Perform the following to detach the policy that has full administrative privileges as found in the audit step: 1. Lists all IAM users, groups, and roles that the specified managed policy is attached to. aws iam list-entities-for-policy --policy-arn <policy_arn> 2. Detach the policy from all IAM Users: aws iam detach-user-policy --user-name <iam_user> --policy-arn <policy_arn> 3. Detach the policy from all IAM Groups: aws iam detach-group-policy --group-name <iam_group> --policy-arn <policy_arn> 4. Detach the policy from all IAM Roles: aws iam detach-role-policy --role-name <iam_role> --policy-arn <policy_arn> References: 1. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html 2. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/IAM/latest/UserGuide/access_policies_managed- vs-inline.html 3. CCE-78912-3 4. https://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/iam/index.html#cli-aws-iam

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- This control relates to IAM policies, which are fully queryable and manageable using AWS APIs, boto3, and the AWS CLI.
- The audit description provides explicit AWS CLI commands to check the policies, which confirms it is programmable.
- IAM is a standard AWS service with API access for checking and managing policies, fitting the automated criteria.
- AWS IAM SDK in boto3 and CLI commands can check for policies with `"Effect": "Allow"`, `"Action": "*"`, and `"Resource": "*"` conditions.

**PROGRAM NAME:** aws_iam_policy_admin_privileges_check

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- **AWS API calls:** Utilize the boto3 SDK for IAM to list and examine policies.
  - Use `boto3.client('iam')` to create an IAM client.
  - Call `list_policies(OnlyAttached=True)` to retrieve all attached IAM policies.
  - For each policy, use `get_policy_version(PolicyArn=<policy_arn>, VersionId=<default_version>)` to retrieve the details of the policy version in use.
  - Inspect the policy statements to find any with `"Effect": "Allow"`, `"Action": "*"`, and `"Resource": "*"`.
- **CLI Alternative:** The AWS CLI can be used as described in the audit section:
  - `aws iam list-policies --only-attached --output text` to list IAM policies.
  - `aws iam get-policy-version --policy-arn <policy_arn> --version-id <version>` to detail versions and check for full administrative privileges.
- **Configuration Properties:** Check if any policy statement grants `"Allow"` permissions for all actions and resources.
- **Pass/Fail Criteria:** 
  - **Pass:** No IAM policy with `"Effect": "Allow"`, `"Action": "*"`, `"Resource": "*"`.
  - **Fail:** At least one such policy is found, requiring removal for compliance.
- **Enumeration:** Examine all IAM policies across the AWS account, ensuring no policies provide unrestricted administrative privileges.
